_comment: Per-instrument source radiation profiles (Phase 2).
These are hand-digitised approximations of polar plots from Meyer,
'Acoustics and the Performance of Music' (Springer, 2009 English ed.,
ch. 4-7), and Pollow et al. (Aachen 2012) where the published 1/3-
octave data could be averaged into our 8 octave bands.
The model is a per-band cosine-power formula:
g(θ, b) = floor[b] + (1 - floor[b]) * max(0, 0.5 + 0.5*cos(θ))^exp[b]
exp[b] controls how quickly the polar pattern narrows with frequency;
floor[b] sets the rear-radiation floor (a true cardioid would be 0;
real instruments have a non-zero floor, typically -15..-25 dB).
Each entry is loaded into the SourceRadiation registry at plugin
startup (PluginProcessor::loadInstrumentRadiationJson) and appears in
the radiation dropdown in the source panel.
Optional 'defaultTiltDeg' (v2.12+, default 0): the typical playing
elevation the source is held at, in degrees. Positive = aimed upward
(e.g. violin/viola = +30, tuba = +90, voice = +10, brass forward = 0).
Used by the UI to auto-populate the tilt slider when a preset is
picked; the actual per-source tilt always lives on IRSynthParams::
spkl_tilt / spkr_tilt and is fully under user control.
To add a new instrument: add an entry below, save the file, rebuild
(BinaryData regenerates from Resources/) — no engine code changes.

instruments.name: Trumpet
_notes: LF below ~500 Hz omni; bell narrows aggressively above 1 kHz.
Rear floor decreases sharply with frequency (small bell vs
wavelength). At 4 kHz the back is ~25 dB down from front.

instruments.name: Trombone
instruments._source: Meyer 2009, fig 5.10 (tenor trombone, slide-out polar diagrams)
_notes: Similar to trumpet but slightly broader lobe at HF (larger bell).
LF behaviour very similar; HF ~10% wider main lobe.

instruments.name: Tuba
_notes: Tuba bell points UP. v2.12 defaults the source tilt to +90° so
the bell radiates straight up by default. The bandExp/bandFloor
values describe the cardioid-shaped lobe relative to the BELL'S
axis (not horizontal); combined with tilt = +90° this puts the
narrowing at the ceiling. Users wanting the v2.11 2D-broad
approximation can drag the tilt back to 0°.

instruments.name: Violin
_notes: Real violin radiation is highly upward-tilted (instrument body
faces ceiling). With v2.12 tilt, the front-of-body axis points
+30° upward by default (typical playing posture). The horizontal
lobe shape (bandExp/bandFloor) is then re-aimed up via tilt so
ceiling-direction reflections are emphasised.

instruments.name: Cello
_notes: Body radiates forward and somewhat upward (the front is angled
up about 20° in normal playing posture). v2.12 defaults to +20°.
Pattern broader than violin at LF (larger body), narrowing
similarly at HF.

instruments.name: Voice (alto)
_notes: Forward bias above ~700 Hz (formant region). LF is omni due to
throat radiation. Floor stays relatively high — singers radiate
audibly behind themselves. Slightly broader than 'Voice' generic.
v2.12 defaults to +10° tilt (mouth angled slightly up when
projecting).

instruments.name: Voice (tenor)
_notes: Slightly more forward-projecting than alto due to lower-formant
energy travelling further; HF narrowing similar.

instruments.name: Flute
_notes: Bidirectional (lengthwise) at LF — modelled as fairly broad with
high rear floor since the head joint and foot joint both radiate.
Narrows somewhat at HF; never as narrow as brass.

instruments.name: Clarinet
_notes: Bell-radiating reed, narrower than flute but broader than brass.
Held at ~45° from vertical in playing posture; v2.12 defaults
tilt to +20° to capture this.

instruments.name: Oboe
_notes: Conical reed, narrower polar than clarinet at HF due to small bell.
Broader at LF. Generally very forward-projecting in concert-hall
use which is why it carries so well. Held at ~30° from vertical.
